properties.Name: June 9 — 1 Million March
properties.Description: 1 million people march against extradition bill through Central and Admiralty

properties.Name: June 12 — LegCo Siege
properties.Description: Protesters surround LegCo — police use tear gas for first time

properties.Name: June 16 — 2 Million March
properties.Description: 2 million march — largest protest in Hong Kong history

properties.Name: July 1 — LegCo Stormed
properties.Description: Protesters break into and occupy LegCo — symbolic reclaiming of legislature

properties.Name: July 7 — Kowloon March
properties.Description: Movement spreads to Kowloon — Mong Kok becomes protest front

properties.Name: July 21 — Yuen Long Attack
properties.Description: Mob attacks protesters and civilians at Yuen Long MTR station

properties.Name: August 12 — Airport Occupation
properties.Description: Protesters occupy departure hall — hundreds of flights cancelled. Movement goes international.

properties.Name: November 12 — CUHK Battle
properties.Description: Police fire tear gas on CUHK campus — students defend university bridge

properties.Name: November 18 — PolyU Siege
properties.Description: Police besiege Polytechnic University for 11 days — hundreds trapped inside

properties.Name: June 30 — NSL Passed
properties.Description: National Security Law passed — open protest effectively criminalized. Movement ends in public form.
